messages.content: You are a practical, friendly assistant for everyday how-to advice.
My kitchen faucet drips constantly after I shut it off. It’s a single-handle faucet. Can I fix this myself and what are the steps?

Yes—most single-handle drips are from a worn cartridge or O-rings. Here’s a safe DIY approach:

Tools and parts
- Replacement cartridge (match your faucet brand/model), plumber’s grease, adjustable wrench, screwdriver/Allen key, needle-nose pliers, towels, and a sink plug. Optional: penetrating oil, masking tape for protecting finishes.

Prep
1) Shut off water: Turn off the hot and cold stop valves under the sink. Open the faucet to relieve pressure.
2) Plug the drain so small parts don’t fall in. Put a towel in the sink to protect it.

Disassemble
3) Remove handle: Pry off the small index cap, loosen the set screw (Allen) or handle screw, and lift off the handle.
4) Expose cartridge: Remove any decorative dome/cover and the retaining nut or clip. Note orientation and how parts stack (photos help).

Replace cartridge and seals
5) Pull the old cartridge straight up. If stuck, wiggle gently or use pliers; avoid scratching the valve body.
6) Clean the valve body. Lightly coat new O-rings with plumber’s grease.
7) Install the new cartridge in the same orientation (tabs/keys must align). Reinstall the retaining clip/nut snugly (don’t overtighten).

Reassemble and test
8) Rebuild the handle assembly in reverse order.
9) Slowly open the shutoff valves. Check for leaks while running both hot and cold.
10) If aerator flow is weak or sputters, unscrew the aerator and flush debris, then reinstall.

If it still drips
- Confirm you used the correct cartridge model and orientation.
- Inspect and replace worn O-rings/seat seals if present.
- Mineral buildup may prevent a good seal; soak parts in vinegar and reassemble.

When to call a pro
- Corroded or seized parts, damaged valve body, or persistent leaks at the base after a proper cartridge swap.

This repair typically takes 20–45 minutes once you have the correct cartridge.
